summary: Pure-data transfer of bonfire.workflows from V1. Five factory functions (standard_build, debug, dual_scout, triple_scout, spike) plus a WorkflowRegistry. Adaptations are correct: project_strategist deferred, private refs scrubbed, singular stub deleted. 119 RED tests flip to GREEN, full suite 607 passed. Engineering discipline is tight across every criterion checked.

pytest.note: Bare pytest invocation without PYTHONPATH shows 488 passed + 119 errors because the .venv editable install points at bonfire-public/src (main worktree), not the review worktree. Expected for worktree review; operator correctly documented PYTHONPATH fallback.

c9_runtime_audit.cmd: inspect vars() of every loaded bonfire.workflows.* module for __module__ origins
c9_runtime_audit.result: Only bonfire.models.plan (StageSpec/WorkflowPlan/WorkflowType) leaks in. No engine/dispatch/handlers/events/cli origins.

pydantic_v2_frozen.note: WorkflowPlan declares model_config = ConfigDict(frozen=True, populate_by_name=True) in bonfire.models.plan. Factories produce frozen instances — mutation raises ValidationError (verified in-process).

modern_type_hints.note: from __future__ import annotations used. No typing.Optional or typing.List. Forward refs via TYPE_CHECKING to avoid runtime import of WorkflowPlan/Callable. list[str], dict[str, Callable[..., WorkflowPlan]], str | None idioms throughout.

registry_correctness.note: register raises ValueError with name in message ("Workflow 'beta' is already registered."). get raises KeyError with sorted 'Available: ...' list and from None to suppress implicit chaining. list_names returns list[str] (verified at runtime). __contains__ and __len__ implemented and tested.

get_default_registry_freshness.note: Builds WorkflowRegistry() anew each call, registers 5 factories. Runtime verified: r1 is not r2, mutation of r1 does not leak into r2. No module-level singleton.

factory_signatures.note: All 5 factories take zero parameters and return WorkflowPlan (inspect.signature confirmed). Matches locked Sage types.

research_parallel_topology.note: Single parallel_group f'{name}_scouts' via _multi_scout_workflow. Sage built with depends_on=scout_names — fans in on every scout. Spike has no knight/warrior/prover (only scouts + sage via the same helper).

standard_build_warrior_shape.note: max_iterations=3, on_gate_failure defaults to None (self-bounce prevention — prover and wizard route failures to warrior, but warrior itself does not). gates=['test_pass'], depends_on=['knight']. Matches V1 contract and Sage lock.

c9_import_discipline.note: src/bonfire/workflows/ imports only from bonfire.models.plan. Parametrized TestDependencyConstraints covers 5 forbidden prefixes (engine/dispatch/handlers/events/cli). Runtime audit of vars() confirms no forbidden bindings.

findings.title: standard_build docstring says Bard writes the PR and Wizard reviews it, but plan has no bounce on Bard
findings.detail: The narrative docstring implies a review loop anchored on Bard, but only wizard.on_gate_failure='warrior' exists. That is the V1 shape (and matches the Sage contract and test_wizard_bounces_to_warrior), so the code is correct. The docstring is accurate but worth keeping an eye on: it conflates Bard's role (PR authorship) with Wizard's gate (review_approval). Not a behavioral issue.
findings.recommendation: No change. Flag for future doc-pass if the workflow evolves.

findings.title: get() could raise a more typed error than bare KeyError
findings.detail: get() raises KeyError with a human-readable message including sorted available names. This matches Sage lock and V1. A dedicated subclass (e.g., UnknownWorkflowError(KeyError)) would let callers catch specifically without pattern-matching message text, but that is beyond BON-336's transfer scope and V1 does not do it either.
findings.recommendation: Defer. File a follow-up ticket only if a consumer actually needs type-level differentiation.

findings.title: pytest.raises(Exception) with noqa: B017 catches too broadly
findings.detail: TestFactoryInvariants.test_plan_is_frozen and TestStandardBuild.test_plan_is_frozen use pytest.raises(Exception) to accept Pydantic's ValidationError. Runtime check confirms ValidationError is raised. Tightening to pytest.raises(ValidationError) via `from pydantic import ValidationError` would document intent, but the current form is robust against Pydantic version changes that might rename the error class.
findings.recommendation: Accept as-is. The breadth is deliberate for forward-compat with Pydantic.

findings.title: get_default_registry() uses local imports — intentional cycle-break, worth a comment
findings.detail: The imports inside get_default_registry are local (not module-level) to sidestep the circular nature of the design: registry.py knows about the factories, but the package __init__ imports both registry and the factory modules. Lifting these to module-level would also work (no cycle in practice because factories don't import registry), but V1 keeps them local. Fine either way; a one-line comment would document intent.
findings.recommendation: Optional: add `# Local import: keeps this module factory-agnostic at module load time` above the imports.

findings.title: TestDependencyConstraints inspects vars() — misses conditional / TYPE_CHECKING imports
findings.detail: The C9 test scans vars(mod).values() for __module__ origins. This catches real bindings but would NOT detect a TYPE_CHECKING-only import of, say, bonfire.engine (which is fine — typing-only is harmless at runtime). A future tightening could grep the source for `from bonfire.engine` string literals, but that adds fragility. Current design is correct for the C9 contract as written.
findings.recommendation: No change. Runtime binding check is the right C9 lens.

findings.title: spike() and dual_scout() both call _multi_scout_workflow(scout_count=2) — identical shapes except for name/description
findings.detail: Spike and dual_scout produce structurally identical plans (2 scouts + sage), differing only in name and description. This is intentional V1 behavior: spike is a semantic alias emphasizing 'no implementation', not a structural variant. TestSpike.test_has_scouts uses `>= 2`, allowing future widening without a breaking change. Good composition.
findings.recommendation: No change. Worth noting in session handoff that spike == dual_scout structurally in v0.1.

recommendation: Approve and merge. All eight engineering-discipline criteria pass with verified evidence. No critical or important findings. The six suggestions are all deferrable or informational — none block the transfer. 607/607 tests pass with PYTHONPATH set to the worktree src, ruff clean, C9 runtime audit confirms only bonfire.models.plan binds into workflows submodules.
